subject: [Pre-read] CB FP&A monthly Initiatives and Update_Jul'25

content.answer:
Hi Ian and Jianghong,

Pls find tmr's FP&A monthly meeting, given Jun performance quite volatile,
we will share more details on observations of performances drill down in
prep for Chris meeting as well, key agenda as follows


*CB Performance update*

   - Jun’25 Actuals Performance (as of Jun 11th)
   - Jul’25 Projection
   - H1’25 Review
   - Drill down of Jun’25 Performance


*Key Initiatives Updates*

   - All BU: Local Ownership Initiatives (Workshop Feedback)
   - Marketplace: Commission Increase/Waiver Updates
   - SLS: [VN/PH] Customs New Regulations Status Update
   - SLS: [MY] BSC Push Impact


*Faye Yan*

Shopee | Cross Border

*Mobile:* +65 87767030

*Address:* 5 Science Park Drive, Shopee Building, Singapore 118265

Slide 17: For such analysis, usually CNCB side only focus on those SKUs
with orders in the past x (usually 6) months. Is that how you pull data?

Slide 18: Think Derek and Elaine also have some analysis on this, but more
on take rate changes. Can get from them?

Slide 25: I asked Alfred to pull out product data to show how many churn
users during KYC process for KR/JP/HK CB now. Overall, my sense is the
impact is not so bad.

content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 6

   - Can remind me where did we land on the rates for NN WH?

And what is
   our view of this WH moving forward?
- And how is the utilization of NN WH right now?

How is the P&L of this
   WH looking like?

Slide 4

   - How is the accuracy of our KRJP projections now?

Slide 7

   - Is this view for CN business?
Or entire MP?
- Is there a separate view for KR business?

Slide 9

   - What is the reason that SCS does not benefit from all these recent PRM
   push?

Slide 16

   - MY did not see an increase in FBS % even though we raised 3PF rates?

Slide 17

   - How do I compare price increase % vs commissions % increase?

How are
   we negotiating to get sellers to reduce price?

Slide 18

   - Do we know what is causing the issue in VN here?

Slide 20

   - Think we need to take a step back and evaluate the use case of NNWH?
- What does it do for TH business?
- What does it do for VN business?
- For TH business, how does it compare vs Anbo WH?

Is it worth
   sacrificing USD 750K/month?
- For VN business, how does it fit in our overall 3PF/FBS strategy?

What
   will the comms be vs 3PF?

Will we end up pushing too much to FBS, when we
   should be pushing them towards 3PF instead?
- For our NNWH, how long more of a lease do we have?

And how much does
   it cost/month?

I think we should not be forced to make the wrong decision
   for the business just because we have a fix cost that we are managing

platform fees increase (seller needs to mark-up price to counter this), and

      ADO increase more, benefiting from FSV0 push (FSV0% increased more

      -

      Meanwhile, similar as PH, VN also push for voucher related but Choice

      1.9/3.8/7.6)

Hi Ian,

Regarding yesterday’s Chris meeting P&L question, pls find below answer (i
sent here for CB review first, and only keep Choice members)

#1 Standalone P&L in MP business session confusion


#2 For choice, why seller P&L is positive, but EBITDA is so negative

reflected in the seller P&L (+0.5mil) in Jun’25, the gap is due to

1) -4.2mil from SLS discount (31% discount on ASF vs other sellers)

2) -0.8mil from MP commission discount (total 4% discount, 2% from Anbo
rate lower than SLS sellers applied to Choice-SLS, another 2% from Dec’24
Chris alignment on mgmt reporting - applied to all Choice)

3) *-**1*.3mil from MP ads discount (50% off, also per Dec’24 Chris
alignment on mgmt reporting)

To further explain the run-down of how Choice mgmt. EBITDA is calculated

Definition clarity:

   -

   EBITDA (per meeting deck) reflects Choice as a business contribution to
   SHP EBITDA (i.e. in Jun, our CB biz has a overall EBITDA of 10mil, with
   choice contributing -6mil, rest of BU contributes +16mil), this includes A)
   Choice own standalone P&L, Choice as a biz contribution to the platform B)
   MP P&L, C) SLS P&L, and D) shared cost allocation, this definition
has been aligned
   with Reg FP&A reporting to Chris
   -

   However, this definition is different from Choice own entity EBITDA (so
   call seller EBITDA), which is used solely to evaluate Choice as a
   seller’s earning/loss when operating its business, the equation shall be
   seller P&L - interest - tax - depreciation - amortization, which is the
   version that Lining showed to G-team Oct’24


Rundown of EBITDA(SHP Mgmt. EBITDA) calculation
[image: image.png]


Best,
*Faye Yan*
